prerequisites: A running TiDB cluster (self-managed TiDB or TiDB Cloud) reachable from where pm runs.
The cluster host address (host) and the MySQL protocol port (port), which defaults to 4000 on self-managed TiDB.
The name of the database (database) you want to read from.
A TiDB user account (username) with a password (password) that has read access (for example SELECT) on the target database.

If the cluster is not directly reachable, an SSH bastion host for the SSH tunnel (tunnel_method), with either an SSH private key (tunnel_method.ssh_key) or an SSH user password (tunnel_method.tunnel_user_password).
If connecting over TLS, the server must have TLS enabled (ssl-cert / ssl-key configured) so you can set ssl on the connection.

authMethods.name: Username and password (MySQL-compatible)
authMethods.summary: TiDB authenticates clients with a user name and password over the MySQL protocol. Create the account with CREATE USER 'user'@'host' IDENTIFIED BY '...' and grant read privileges with GRANT; TiDB hashes and stores the password in the mysql.user system table, mirroring MySQL. Provide these as the username config field and the password secret field.

authMethods.name: SSH tunnel key or password (optional)
authMethods.summary: When the database is not directly reachable, pm can tunnel the connection through an SSH bastion (tunnel_method). Authenticate to the bastion with an SSH private key (tunnel_method.ssh_key) or with the SSH user's password (tunnel_method.tunnel_user_password). This secures the network path to TiDB and is separate from the database login itself.

setupSteps.title: Create a TiDB user with read access
setupSteps.body: Connect to the cluster with an existing admin account and create a dedicated login, for example CREATE USER 'pm_reader'@'%' IDENTIFIED BY 'your-password'; then grant read access with GRANT SELECT ON your_database.* TO 'pm_reader'@'%';. TiDB follows the MySQL user-account model, so the account is identified by user name and client host.

setupSteps.title: Gather connection details
setupSteps.body: Note the cluster host (host) and the MySQL protocol port (port). On self-managed TiDB the default port is 4000. Set database to the schema you want to sync, and set username and password to the credentials created above.

setupSteps.title: Decide on TLS
setupSteps.body: If the TiDB server has TLS enabled (ssl-cert and ssl-key configured, PEM format), set the ssl option on the connection. TiDB accepts TLS and non-TLS clients on the same port, so enabling ssl encrypts the connection without changing the port.

setupSteps.title: Configure an SSH tunnel if needed
setupSteps.body: If TiDB is not directly reachable from pm, set tunnel_method to route through an SSH bastion. Provide the SSH private key (tunnel_method.ssh_key) or the SSH user password (tunnel_method.tunnel_user_password) for the bastion host.

setupSteps.title: Set optional JDBC parameters
setupSteps.body: Use jdbc_url_params to append extra JDBC connection options as key=value pairs (separated by &) if you need custom driver behavior. Leave it empty for the defaults.

sources.title: TiDB user account management (CREATE USER, GRANT, password model)
Enable TLS between TiDB clients and servers
Command line flags for TiDB configuration (default MySQL port 4000)
